when_to_use: golden-era boom-bap / jazz rap, sample-chopped Rhodes loop, Dilla push-pull timing, muted horn hook, 75-95 BPM

defaults.feel: humanized 16th grid, kick pushed ahead, snare dragged behind, 54-62% hi-hat swing

signature_moves: golden-era loop: Dm9 -> G13 -> Cmaj9 -> A7(b9,b13)
chill descending loop: Fmaj9 -> Em7 -> Dm9 -> Cmaj9
dark urban minor: Am9 -> D9 -> Fmaj7 -> E7alt
rootless shells: voice only 3rd+7th+extensions, never the root (see hiphop-jazz-genre.md SS4) - bass alone holds root
cadence_options: loop turnaround: land on an altered dominant (A7b9b13 or E7alt) that spins back to bar 1
no functional final cadence needed - the loop simply drops out at the outro (see arrangement_defaults)
avoid: voicing the root on Rhodes/piano while bass also plays it - creates low-end mud (see rootless voicings, hiphop-jazz-genre.md SS4)
quantized/on-grid drums - this genre's identity is the push-pull, not precision (see groove_profile)

hook_archetypes.name: muted-horn-riff
hook_archetypes.rhythm: a short 1-2 bar phrase landing at the end of a 4-bar loop, not a continuous line
hook_archetypes.contour: a smoky, unhurried phrase (Harmon-muted trumpet or tenor sax) resolving into the next loop's downbeat

hook_archetypes.name: vocal-chop-accent
hook_archetypes.rhythm: a short sampled vocal landing used as a rhythmic accent, not a melodic statement
hook_archetypes.contour: a single word/syllable placed on an off-beat or loop turnaround
hook_archetypes.first_appears: Hook/Chorus, layered under the horn riff

intro.note_lengths: sparse, filtered - the loop is muffled (low-pass engaged), no lead yet

verse.note_lengths: the rap flow or lead line sits back, unhurried, few sustained notes
verse.placement: laid-back per the dilla-boom-bap vocal/lead offset - the most dragged voice in the mix

hook.note_lengths: short muted-horn phrase (1-2 bar) plus vocal-chop accents
hook.contour: smoky, understated - never a long virtuosic line
hook.placement: laid-back, landing at the end of the 4-bar loop

breakdown.note_lengths: sparse - drums drop out, only the loop and a soloist (flute/sax) remain
breakdown.contour: open space, a contrast moment before the hook returns
breakdown.placement: rubato-adjacent, loosest placement in the arrangement

drum_skeleton.low_energy: standard boom-bap 1-bar pattern (see hiphop-jazz-genre.md SS6): kick on 1 and the 'and' of 2/3, snare on 3 with a ghost note, hi-hat steady 8ths
drum_skeleton.mid_energy: hi-hat fills to steady 16ths, ghost snares thicken, occasional kick pickup
drum_skeleton.high_energy: denser ghost-note snare pattern, hi-hat opens on accents, still never quantized - push-pull stays audible even at the densest point

arrangement_defaults.layout_rules: intro states the filtered Rhodes loop alone with vinyl crackle/ambience; filter opens and drums+sub-bass enter together for Verse 1; drums drop briefly every 8 bars for a breath; hook brings in the muted-horn riff and vocal chops over the same loop; verse 2 swaps sub-bass for a sampled-upright character; bridge strips to loop+sub+solo only; outro re-engages the low-pass filter and fades under rising vinyl crackle

anti_boredom_rules: drums must never sound quantized - the push-pull (kick ahead, snare behind) must be audible at every energy level
the harmonic loop stays 2-4 bars and hypnotic - do not develop it into a longer progression; vary texture/filtering/layering instead (see hiphop-jazz-genre.md SS8, SS10)
at least one full drum drop (breakdown) must occur before the final hook/outro
the muted-horn-riff hook must recur varied by register or response phrase, never copy-pasted identically
keep the root off the harmony instrument at all times - rootless voicings only, bass alone holds root
